title: ICT Support Engineer - Level 3
description: PFH Technology are currently looking for an **ICT Support Engineer \- Level 3** to join our growing team. **Location:** Dublin 1, hybrid **Contract length**: 12\-months (with expected extensions) **Responsibilities:** The ideal candidate will have at least **10\-years’ experience** in the following: * Configure and manage Microsoft Entra ID (Azure AD) for identity governance, conditional access, and MFA. * Implement Privileged Identity Management (PIM) and role\-based access controls. * Deploy and manage Microsoft Intune for device compliance, application deployment, and endpoint configuration. * Configure \& manage Microsoft Defender XDR solutions (Defender for Endpoint, Defender for Office 365 etc). * Monitor and respond to security alerts using Microsoft Sentinel and other SIEM tools. * Apply best practices for securing Azure resources, including network security groups, firewalls, and encryption. * Implement Zero Trust principles across identity, devices, and applications. * Lead investigations for security incidents and provide remediation strategies. * Maintain detailed documentation for configurations, policies, and procedures. * Must be able to show a high degree of awareness of cybersecurity best practices and concepts including phishing awareness, Identity and Access Management, Multifactor Authentication, Zero Trust Security Model, Purview specially Information Protection * Must have demonstrated experience of threat evaluation and security incident response * Must have exposure to and be able to demonstrate experience using an ITIL aligned Service desk and associated principles such as Incident and Service request management, Problem Management, Change Management, continual Service improvement etc. * Must be able to demonstrate excellent support \& troubleshooting skills for Level 3 Incident Management aligned with ITIL best practices (Incident management, Service Request and Problem Management). **Duties:** * Be willing and capable to work well within a team environment * Be able to clearly communicate technical solutions in a user friendly and professional manner demonstrating a customer service mind\-set * Provide third line support for ICT Operations and other ICT Teams * Liaise with other ICT technical team specialists within the tenderer’s organisation to complete projects, (the response should clearly detail the areas where the tenderer has technical expertise). * Be able to follow Incident Management Process. * Create and update our Knowledge Management system, SOP’s, and Runbooks. * To contribute effectively to organisational and team processes and adhere to policies including but not limited to Data Protection and Information Security * Be responsible for ensuring the IT systems and applications are maintained to best practice. * Recognise system deficiencies and suggest effective solutions while complying with company policies and procedures. * Research current best practice technology solutions and translate them into our environment * To participate in Change Advisory Board (CAB) and Problem Management meetings and to share information with the support teams. * Be responsible for resolution of all tickets within agreed KPI timelines. * Carry out any other duties as required. **Education:** Must hold at least one of the following but all three would be advantageous: * Microsoft Certified: Azure Security Engineer Associate AZ\-500 * Microsoft Certified: Security Operations Analyst Associate SC\-200 * Microsoft Certified: Identity and Access Administrator Associate SC\-300 * Microsoft Certified: Endpoint Administrator Associate MD\-102 PFH Technology, owned by Ricoh – a Japanese based global ICT company, is a premier provider of end\-to\-end ICT solutions and a managed services portfolio scaling from SMEs to large Enterprise organisations. We have unrivalled vendor relationships. We can procure, design, deploy and support all your ICT needs. Our ISO certified Custodian™ Cloud Services and Custodian™ Managed Services provide the technology and expertise to mitigate risk and reduce your costs immediately. We have a nationwide network of over 750 dedicated professionals, ready to meet your ICT needs, with offices in Dublin, Cork and Galway. We are an equal opportunity employer and value diversity at PFH. We do not discriminate on the basis of age, disability, civil or family status, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, race, religion, or any other legally protected status. We are committed to ensuring that individuals with disabilities or access needs are provided with reasonable accommodation during the recruitment process, to perform essential job functions, and to access the full benefits of employment. Please contact us if you require accommodation at any stage. IND1

title: ITSM Process Owner - Incident & Problem Management
description: Because we strive to put people first. Culture, our way. **Technology at Primark** What’s a career at Primark all about? The positive impact you’re making, the experiences you’re having and the people you’re with. You’re our inspiration. Embrace what sets you apart, own your career and develop in ways you never expected. Leave your mark. And do it your way. Our technology team is actively shaping the next wave of advancements. Engaged with innovative initiatives, your expertise will propel our business into the future. Collaborating with a creative team of tech enthusiasts, you’ll contribute your unique skills to fuel our technological advancements. **What You’ll Do as** **ITSM Process Owner \- Incident \& Problem Management** In your role, you’ll collaborate with different people across a range of skillsets. Here’s a flavour of your day\-to\-day: * Own the end\-to\-end governance, consistency, and continual improvement of one or more ITIL process domains across Primark’s technology estate, ensuring alignment with business needs, ITSM strategy, and compliance across internal teams and third‑party partners, reporting to the Service Assurance Lead. * Act as the accountable owner for assigned ITIL processes (e.g., Incident, Problem, Change Management), defining, documenting, and maintaining policies, standards, workflows, and procedures while ensuring consistent adoption, governance, and compliance against agreed KPIs and SLAs. * Serve as the subject matter expert and escalation point for process\-related issues, supporting integration with ITSM tools such as ServiceNow, identifying optimisation and automation opportunities, and collaborating with Service Desk, Service Owners, Transition Managers, Product Teams, and Engineering to ensure operational supportability. * Define, maintain, and analyse process metrics and KPIs, lead performance and compliance reviews, and deliver regular dashboards and reports to the Service Assurance Lead and stakeholders, highlighting trends, risks, insights, and improvement opportunities. * Identify, prioritise, and implement Continual Service Improvement initiatives to improve maturity, efficiency, and service quality; facilitate improvement workshops and reviews; track progress and outcomes, ensuring alignment with broader ITSM and organisational objectives. * Engage and collaborate with internal service teams, product and engineering functions, and third‑party providers to drive process adherence and shared accountability, promote awareness of process value and behaviours, and support training and change communication initiatives. **What You’ll Get** People are at the heart of what we do here, so it’s essential we provide you with the right environment to perform at your very best. Let’s talk lifestyle: Healthcare, pension, and potential bonus. 27 days of leave, plus bank holidays and if you want, you can buy 5 more. Because Primark is all about tailoring to you, we offer Tax Saver Tickets, fitness centre, and a subsidised cafeteria. **What You’ll Bring** Here at Primark, we want everyone to feel valued – so please bring your authentic self to work, of course with some other key experience and abilities for this role in particular: * 4\+ years of experience in IT Service Management with proven responsibility for managing or owning ITIL processes, supported by a strong understanding of ITIL v4 (or v3\) in enterprise and multi‑supplier environments. * Demonstrated experience implementing, governing, and improving ITSM processes across internal teams and external providers, including managed service providers and SIAM or multi‑vendor operating models. * Proficiency in ITSM platforms such as ServiceNow and associated reporting tools, with the ability to monitor performance, compliance, and improvement opportunities. * Strong analytical and problem‑solving capability, using data‑driven insights to support decision‑making, process optimisation, and continual improvement initiatives. * Excellent communication, collaboration, and stakeholder engagement skills, with the ability to influence process adoption, compliance, and behavioural change across diverse teams. * Desirable experience including ITIL v4 Managing Professional or Intermediate certification, retail or distributed business environments, leading CSI or process maturity assessments, and familiarity with Agile or DevOps ways of working and their integration with ITSM processes. Does this sound like you? Great, because we can’t wait to see what you’ll bring. You’ll be supported within a team of equally capable people, celebrating who you are and aiding you reach your potential. At Primark, we’re excited about our future \- and we’re excited to develop yours. **About Primark** At Primark, people matter. They’re the beating heart of our business and the reason we’ve grown from our first store in Dublin in 1969 to a £9bn\+ turnover business and over 80,000 colleagues and over 440 stores in 17 countries today. Our values run through everything we do. In essence, we're Caring and always strive to put people first. We're also Dynamic, bravely pushing the boundaries to stay ahead. And finally, we succeed Together. If you need any reasonable adjustments or have an accessibility request, during your recruitment journey, such as extended time or breaks between online assessments, a sign language interpreter, mobility access, or assistive technology please contact your talent acquisition specialist. All offers of employment are subject to background checks, including right to work, reference education and for some roles criminal, and financial checks. If you have any concerns, please reach out to our talent acquisition team to discuss. **REQ ID:** JR\-8231

title: Independent Consultant in M&A
description: You already have the network. We have the opportunities to match it with. Hetmos Group is a boutique organization active across three premium verticals: M\&A for small and lower mid\-cap companies, strategic and off\-market real estate, and wealth structuring for executives, entrepreneurs, and investors. We are building a community of Independent Mandate Partners to represent our activities within their own ecosystem. *What this partnership looks lik* e You introduce us to high\-value contacts: CEOs, shareholders, investors, HNWIs, strategic partners. You identify opportunities in M\&A, real estate, or wealth. You initiate the relationship. We handle qualification, structuring, negotiation, closing. You are paid a significant commission at closing. No execution on your side. No operational load. Just intelligent, targeted introductions. *The right profile* • Experienced : consultants, bankers, brokers, advisors, lawyers, accountants, family office professionals, senior executives with an active network of decision\-makers • Junior : welcome if introduced, with strong relational skills and real access to qualified people M\&A, real estate, or wealth expertise is not required. We train. *What we offer* • 100% success\-based compensation, uncapped commissions • Independent contractor status, complete freedom • Ability to operate across one, two, or all three verticals • Experienced in\-house execution team • Premium positioning that reflects well on your introductions *Long\-term upside* Top performers access larger mandates, can structure a territory, and may be invited to equity participation. I *nterested?* If your network deserves more than coffees and small talk, talk to us.

title: Industrial/Window Cleaning Operatives
description: Agua\-Clean Services is a professional industrial cleaning contractor specialising in commercial window cleaning and high\-level access services throughout Ireland. We deliver reliable, safety\-driven, and environmentally responsible cleaning solutions to clients across the commercial, industrial, and retail sectors. Due to continued growth, we are seeking motivated and reliable Industrial Window Cleaning Operatives to join our team. **Key Responsibilities:** * Carry out professional window cleaning and industrial cleaning services at client sites across Dublin and nationwide. * Work safely at height, including use of MEWPs (where certified). * Operate both independently and as part of a skilled, safety\-focused team. * Maintain excellent on\-site communication and customer service standards. * Comply with all company health, safety, and environmental procedures. * Adapt to flexible working hours, including early mornings, evenings, weekends, and overtime as required. **Candidate Requirements:** * Full\-time availability with flexibility for evenings, weekends, and overtime. * Strong punctuality, reliability, and readiness for early starts. * Ability to work independently and as part of a team. * Professional, friendly attitude with strong customer service skills. * Safe Pass and Manual Handling certification preferred (training can be arranged). * Non\-EEA candidates must hold a valid full\-time work permit for Ireland. * Full Category B Driving Licence desirable (driving roles available for eligible candidates). Previous commercial window cleaning experience is an advantage but not essential, as full training will be provided. **What We Offer:** * **Day rates from €134–€167**, depending on experience and duties performed.. * Regular overtime opportunities. * Comprehensive training and structured upskilling pathways. * Career progression opportunities, including training toward Rope Access roles. * Company vehicle available for eligible candidates. * Benefits package including: * Bike to Work Scheme * Employee Assistance Programme * Sick Pay Scheme Job Type: Full\-time Pay: €35,000\.00\-€43,464\.00 per year Benefits: * Bike to work scheme * Employee assistance program Language: * English (required) Licence/Certification: * B Driving Licence (required) Work Location: On the road

title: Infrastructure Software Developer
description: **Job Summary** We are looking for an **Infrastructure Software Developer** passionate about **AI\-driven automation** to join our WorldQuant Aligned Infrastructure team. The successful candidate will deliver high\-quality, scalable infrastructure solutions and self\-service tools for our growing internal users — with AI at the center of how they think, build, and ship. **Responsibilities** * Develop, test and implement infrastructure solutions and self\-service tools, leveraging AI\-assisted development as a default workflow * Develop and maintain scalable internal APIs, CLIs, MCPs and packages * Design and implement CI/CD pipelines, including AI\-augmented testing and deployment validation * Monitor and manage complex distributed systems — automating alerts and implementing self\-healing solutions with AI\-driven observability and remediation * Perform AI\-assisted code reviews and promote best practices around responsible AI adoption * Optimize for async collaboration with our global team **Ideal Candidate** * An exceptional infrastructure software developer committed to high quality code and infrastructure solutions * AI\-native by mindset — already using AI tools (Copilot, ChatGPT, Claude, Cursor, custom agents, etc.) daily because they cannot imagine working without them * A collaborative team player with superior communication and problem\-solving skills * Driven by curiosity, always exploring new technologies — especially emerging AI capabilities **Required Skills / Experience** * Experience as a Systems Engineer, Infrastructure Developer or similar role — years of experience matter far less than demonstrated capability and passion * Proficiency in Python or Go * Experience with IaC tools such as Ansible, SaltStack and Terraform * Hands\-on experience with API development and integration * Familiarity with container orchestration, especially Kubernetes * Experience designing and implementing CI/CD pipelines * Deep understanding of complex distributed systems architectures * Demonstrated fluency with AI/ML tools in a development context — AI should already be part of how you work * Excellent problem\-solving skills and ability to communicate complex concepts clearly **Nice to Have** * Experience building or integrating AI agents for infrastructure tasks (auto\-remediation, intelligent provisioning, natural language interfaces) * Familiarity with LLM APIs, MCPs, prompt engineering, RAG patterns, or agentic frameworks (LangChain, CrewAI, Google ADK, A2A, etc.)

title: Infrastructure System Engineer
description: Snr Microsoft Infrastructure Engineer (M365/ Entra / Azure) **Job Description:** Our client, a leading organisation, is seeking a Senior Entra / M365 and Azure Engineer to support and enhance their cloud identity and infrastructure environment. The role is for 12 months and its a day rate contract for a large public sector client. The role only requires some ad hoc working on site. The candidate must live in Ireland for the role. **Job Responsibilities** * Design, implement and support Microsoft Entra ID (Azure AD), M365, and Azure cloud solutions * Manage identity and access management (IAM) processes including authentication, authorisation and governance * Support and optimise cloud infrastructure across Azure environments * Lead on security best practices across M365 and Azure platforms * Collaborate with cross\-functional teams to deliver scalable and secure cloud solutions * Troubleshoot and resolve complex technical issues across cloud and identity services * Contribute to cloud strategy, architecture, and continuous improvement initiatives * Work with third\-party vendors and internal stakeholders to deliver key projects **Experience Required** * Minimum 5\+ years’ experience in a similar cloud engineering or infrastructure role * Strong hands\-on experience with Microsoft Entra ID (Azure AD), M365, and Azure * Proven experience managing enterprise\-level cloud environments * Experience with identity and access management, including conditional access and MFA * Strong understanding of cloud security principles and best practices * Experience working in hybrid or remote environments with distributed teams **Desirable Skills** * Experience with automation tools such as PowerShell or Terraform * Knowledge of Azure networking and infrastructure design * Familiarity with compliance and governance frameworks within cloud environments * Exposure to DevOps practices and CI/CD pipelines * Experience supporting large\-scale cloud transformation initiatives **Educational Requirements** * Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, or a related field **HOW TO APPLY:** If you are interested in this role, please apply with your updated CV

title: Installation Engineer
description: **Who We Are** Applied Materials is a global leader in materials engineering solutions used to produce virtually every new chip and advanced display in the world. We design, build and service cutting\-edge equipment that helps our customers manufacture display and semiconductor chips – the brains of devices we use every day. As the foundation of the global electronics industry, Applied enables the exciting technologies that literally connect our world – like AI and IoT. If you want to push the boundaries of materials science and engineering to create next generation technology, join us to deliver material innovation that changes the world. **What We Offer** Location: Dublin,IRL You’ll benefit from a supportive work culture that encourages you to learn, develop, and grow your career as you take on challenges and drive innovative solutions for our customers. We empower our team to push the boundaries of what is possible—while learning every day in a supportive leading global company. Visit our Careers website to learn more. At Applied Materials, we care about the health and wellbeing of our employees. We’re committed to providing programs and support that encourage personal and professional growth and care for you at work, at home, or wherever you may go. Learn more about our benefits. As a Field Service Engineer \[Customer Engineer] at Applied Materials, you serve as the direct liaison with customers, collaborating closely to install, maintain, and upgrade equipment. You'll use digital analytics for troubleshooting and apply basic diagnostic techniques to assess and address technical issues. Your responsibilities include performing preventative and corrective maintenance on various systems such as electrical, vacuum, mechanical, plasma, hydraulic, and gas systems. Additionally, you'll coordinate and communicate directly with customers to ensure smooth operations and exceptional service. In order to support our long\-term development, we are looking for talents to join our teams in Europe (Dresden, Germany / Dublin, Ireland / Grenoble, France). Within our Installation department, we are looking for our **Installation Engineer to support European regions**. The iTeam (Installation team) stands for the Installation of semiconductors production equipment across the European Region (mainly in Germany, ireland, France and Italy). We roll in, assemble, connect, and test the new systems until we hand over to our service team and our customers for the manufacturing of wafers to kick off. Being part of the Install Task Force is more than a job, it is a way of life. As a team **we travel approx. 75 % of our working time**, and also get opportunities to support installations worldwide. This position demands technical expertise, customer focus, team involvement and flexibility to travel. **Full training on our equipment will be provided through mentoring.** **Key Responsibilities** * Perform independently installation tasks (mechanical assembly, electrical connections, robot calibrations), from the integration to the inspection of the tools including troubleshooting in case of issues. * Follow all checklists and procedures and ensure correct procedures and schematics are available prior to performing work. * Test equipment with assistance from senior engineers, existing documentation and diagnostic techniques. * Respond to factory issues by communicating with other engineers and participating in escalation conference calls. * Participate in continuous improvement projects to reduce installation cycle time and costs. * Provide accurate reporting of status, hours worked and non\-conformances. * Adhere to the security policy and quality standards of Applied Materials and its customers. **Skills and Experience required:** * Bachelor or Master degree with technical background (mechanical assembly, equipment installation, maintenance, mechatronics, electronics, electrical, mechanical, or similar...) * Position open to junior profiles. * Troubleshooting skills to solve mechanical and electrical issues during system installation. * Ability to communicate daily pass\-downs and equipment status reports. * Project management skills to control installation project within budget, timeline and installation quality requirements. * Ability to work autonomously. * Good English communication skills both written and verbal. * Good Knowledge of the MS Office (Excel, Powerpoint) * The position requires extensive travelling throughout Europe (75% of the time, depending on overall activity requirements) **Physical Requirements:** This position often requires extensive walking, standing for extended periods of time, working in tight spaces, bending, squatting, twisting, kneeling, reaching, climbing stairs and ladders, working at height, lifting up to 35 lbs., the ability to distinguish between colors and working with electricity, the ability to work in an environment with noise that may be difficult for some individuals with sensitivity to noise, and the use of hand and power tools. Additionally, because this position generally involves working in a clean room, it requires the use of appropriate Personal Protective Equipment such as coveralls, hoods, booties, safety glasses, gloves, respirators, chemical aprons, and face shields. **Additional Information** **Time Type:** Full time**Employee Type:** Assignee / Regular**Travel:** Yes, 75% of the Time**Relocation Eligible:** No Applied Materials is an Equal Opportunity Employer. Qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, national origin, citizenship, ancestry, religion, creed, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, age, disability, veteran or military status, or any other basis prohibited by law.

title: Integration Engineer
description: At U.S. Bank, we’re on a journey to do our best. Helping the customers and businesses we serve to make better and smarter financial decisions, enabling the communities we support to grow and succeed in the right ways, all more confidently and more often—that’s what we call the courage to thrive. We believe it takes all of us to bring our shared ambition to life, and each person is unique in their potential. A career with U.S. Bank gives you a wide, ever\-growing range of opportunities to discover what makes you thrive. Try new things, learn new skills and discover what you excel at—all from Day One. As a wholly owned subsidiary of U.S. Bank, Elavon is committed to building the platforms and ecosystems that help over 1\.5 million customers around the world to achieve their financial goals—no matter what they need. From transaction processing to customer service, to driving innovation and launching new products, we’re building a range of tailored payment solutions powered by the latest technology. As part of our team, you can explore what motivates and energizes your career goals: partnering with our customers, our communities, and each other. We actively uphold transparent and fair hiring practices that support individual opportunity, inclusive culture, and career mobility across all levels of our organisation. We offer meaningful opportunities for growth, a culture of inclusion, and a strong commitment to transparency and integrity in everything we do. **Job Description** **Integration Engineer** We are seeking an experienced API Engineer to design, build, secure, and operate enterprise\-grade APIs that integrate cloud platforms with on‑premise banking application suites. The role focuses on developing reliable and scalable integration services, enabling secure data exchange across channels and systems (core banking, payments, fraud, risk, CRM, digital platforms), while meeting stringent regulatory, availability, and security requirements. You will work closely with solution architects, platform engineers, security teams, and business stakeholders to deliver API products aligned to bank integration patterns, including synchronous REST services and event\-driven integrations. **Direct line reporting:** * Head of Architecture and Core Banking – Europe. * Accountabilities and Responsibilities **API Design \& Delivery** * Design and implement RESTful APIs and integration services connecting cloud services to on‑prem banking systems. * Create reusable API patterns for common banking capabilities (customer, accounts, payments, transactions, entitlements). * Define and maintain API contracts (OpenAPI/Swagger), versioning strategies, and backward compatibility. * Deliver APIs that meet performance, latency, availability, and title: Junior Content Designer
description: **About Us** Droga5 is a highly acclaimed, creatively\-led advertising agency known for producing high\-impact, culture\-shifting work for major global brands. The agency was recently recognised as Global Network of the Year by AdAge. **The Role** We are looking for a highly creative, agile, and culturally clued\-in **Junior Content Designer** to join our fast\-paced Creative Department. In this role, you will work across a variety of brands to conceptualize and craft "thumb\-stopping" social content. This position sits at the intersection of traditional digital design and modern social media creation. You will be expected to deliver high\-quality production at high speeds—jumping seamlessly between designing a static graphic, shooting a quick TikTok, and laying out a beautiful client pitch deck. If you live and breathe social media, understand how to design for the feed, and love turning internet trends into brand moments, this is the role for you. **Key Responsibilities** - Social\-First Content Creation: Design, film, and edit engaging content specifically optimized for Instagram, TikTok, Facebook, and emerging platforms. - Agile \& Reactive Execution: Keep your finger on the pulse of internet culture. Work closely with our creative and strategy teams to spot a trending audio or visual format in the morning and have a fully produced, high\-quality post live by the afternoon. - Visual Storytelling \& Multidisciplinary Design: Move fluidly between various mediums, including static layout design, lightweight motion graphics, and native phone\-shot video production. - Presentation \& Pitch Design: Support the wider team with day\-to\-day client work by translating complex ideas into clear, compelling, and beautifully designed presentation decks. - Brand Guardianship: Ensure all content adheres to the specific brand guidelines, tone of voice, and visual identities of our varied client roster. - Creative Collaboration: Participate in brainstorms, share inspiration from your own social feeds, and collaborate with copywriters, art directors, and account managers to bring campaigns to life. **Skills \& Qualifications** - Experience: 1–2\+ years of experience (including internships or strong freelance work) in digital design, social media content creation, or a similar creative role. - The Adobe Suite: Proficiency in Adobe Creative Suite, specifically Photoshop, Illustrator, Premiere Pro, and basic After Effects. - Native \& Mobile Editing: You are incredibly comfortable behind a smartphone camera. You know CapCut, TikTok, and Instagram in\-platform editing tools like the back of your hand. - Design Fundamentals: A solid grasp of typography, composition, and color theory, with the ability to apply these principles to both social posts and professional presentation formats (PowerPoint, Keynote, or Google Slides). - Trend\-Savvy: A deep, intuitive understanding of social media platforms, algorithms, current trends, and what makes a piece of content shareable. - AI Production: You have experience with AI design tools like ComfyAI, Weavy and Higgsfield and show the eagerness to develop your skill using AI. - Adaptability: Ability to thrive in a fast\-paced environment, manage multiple micro\-projects simultaneously, and meet tight deadlines without compromising on quality. **What’s in it for you:** At Accenture Ireland, in addition to a competitive base salary, you will also have an extensive benefits package which includes pension, private health insurance, discounted gym membership, emergency caring facilities, and 2 days’ leave per year for charitable work of your choice! Flexible working arrangements can be discussed depending on the position. **Does this appeal to you? If so, we wish to hear from you:** Please advise if you have any requirements, so we can investigate into arranging reasonable adjustments to the recruitment process. You can contact us at accessIErecruitment@accenture.com Accenture is an equal opportunities employer and encourages applications from all sections of society and does not discriminate on grounds of race, religion or belief, ethnic or national origin, disability, age, citizenship, marital, domestic or civil partnership status, sexual orientation, or gender identity, or any other basis as protected by applicable law.

With dedicated mentorship and a comprehensive training programme, this position is ideal for someone with early experience in a legal or similar professional environment who is eager to learn, grow, and build a long\-term career. **What You'll Do:** * Provide high\-quality administrative support to a team of busy fee earners * Assist with billing processes and support general financial management tasks * Help manage administrative workflows and ensure efficient use of resources * Develop an understanding of matter management, with increasing responsibility over time * Follow firmwide best practice, risk, and compliance procedures * Support fee earners with business development activities * Build strong working relationships across the team and wider business **Who We're Looking For:** * At least one year’s experience in a legal or comparable professional environment * A proactive approach with a strong willingness to support client needs * Good working knowledge of Microsoft Office (Outlook, Word, Teams, and basic Excel) * Strong organisational skills and attention to detail * Someone eager to learn and develop into a Legal PA through structured training * A collaborative team player who can build effective working relationships **Why Choose Us?** Addleshaw Goddard is a place where you are not just valued but encouraged to reach your full potential. Our culture promotes improvement, growth, and collaboration, making us the natural choice for top\-tier clients. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all our employees. Interested? If this role sounds like your next career step, we'd love to hear from you. Click the Apply button to view the full role profile on our website and start your application!

title: Junior Software Engineer, Platform
description: **At Semperis, our mission is to be a Force for Good. Starting with being a great place to work.** We believe that when people feel valued, supported, and empowered, they do their best work. That’s why we focus on creating an employee experience rooted in purpose, growth, and balance. Semperis has been recognized as one of America’s Fastest\-Growing Cybersecurity Companies by the Inc. 5000, a DUNS 100 Top Startup to Work For, and a multi\-year Inc. Best Workplace awardee. **About the Role** ------------------ We are seeking an exceptional **Junior Engineer** to join our Platform Group, where you will architect and develop shared infrastructure components that power Semperis's enterprise security and identity products. This is a highly collaborative role requiring both technical excellence and strong cross\-functional partnership skills. **What You'll Do** ------------------ As a Junior Software Engineer on our Core Platform team, you will: * **Design and build shared platform components** used across multiple product teams, ensuring scalability, reliability, and maintainability * **Architect distributed systems** using microservices and event\-driven patterns that support enterprise\-scale workloads * **Own critical backend services** from conception through deployment, including code quality, performance optimization, and operational excellence * **Collaborate extensively** with product engineers, DevOps, and architects to define platform capabilities and technical standards * **Mentor junior engineers** through code reviews, pair programming, and technical guidance * **Drive technical initiatives** that improve developer productivity, system observability, and platform resilience * **Contribute to architectural decisions** and establish best practices for backend development across the organization * **Analyze system performance and data patterns** to identify optimization opportunities and inform future platform investments **What You Bring** ------------------ ### **Required Qualifications** * **1\+ years of professional software engineering experience**, with expertise in backend development * **Working proficiency in C\# and .NET Core**, including modern framework features and performance optimization * **Production experience with Kubernetes** and container orchestration in cloud environments (Azure/AWS/GCP preferred) * **Experience developing shared libraries, frameworks, or platform components** consumed by multiple teams * **Demonstrated ability building enterprise SaaS applications** serving high\-volume, multi\-tenant environments * **Strong collaborative mindset** with excellent communication skills and experience working across teams to drive consensus * **Bachelor's degree in Computer Science** or equivalent practical experience * **Fluency in English** (written and verbal) ### **Preferred Qualifications** * Experience in the **Enterprise Identity and Access Management (IAM)** domain * Hands\-on expertise with **Kafka, RabbitMQ, or similar message brokers** * Knowledge of **ElasticSearch** or other distributed search/analytics platforms * **Open\-source contributions** or experience maintaining shared component libraries * Experience with **observability tools** (Prometheus, Grafana, distributed tracing) * Background in **API design and governance** for platform services * Familiarity with **CI/CD pipelines** and infrastructure\-as\-code practices **Why Join Semperis?** You’ll be part of a global team on the front lines of cybersecurity innovation. At Semperis, we celebrate curiosity, integrity, and people who take initiative. If you’re someone who sees the glass as half full, embraces challenges as growth opportunities, and values a healthy balance between work and life—we’d love to meet you. **\*\***Semperis maintains office locations in several cities across the globe. Candidates who reside within 45 miles of one of our offices—or where the job description specifies a required location—will follow our hybrid work model. This includes working onsite some days per week and remotely the remaining days. ### **Semperis is an equal opportunity employer and will not discriminate against an applicant or employee based on race, color, religion, creed, national origin or ancestry, ethnicity, sex (including gender, pregnancy, sexual orientation, and gender identity), age, physical or mental disability, veteran or military status, genetic information, citizenship, marital status, or any other legally recognized protected basis under federal, state, or local law. The information collected by the Semperis application is solely to determine suitability for employment, verify identity, and maintain employment statistics.** ### ### **Applicants with disabilities may be entitled to reasonable accommodation under the Americans with Disabilities Act and/or other applicable state or local laws. A reasonable accommodation is a change in the way things are normally done which will ensure an equal employment opportunity without imposing undue hardship on Semperis. Please inform Semperis representative Anna Taylor, Director of Global Recruiting, if you need assistance completing this application or to otherwise participate in the application process.**

title: Junior Valeter
description: Pilsen Auto ŠKODA – Ireland’s Flagship ŠKODA dealership is seeking to further strengthen our team with an enthusiastic, motivated Junior Valeter. This is an excellent opportunity for a motivated individual to join our team. All Training will be given. This job will be on site \& will require a full clean driving licence. *Starting Salary: €30,000\.00 per year.* **Key Responsibilities \& Duties** * Interior Detailing: Vacuuming interiors, shampooing seats and carpets, cleaning upholstery, leather treatment, and cleaning dashboard/trim. * Exterior Cleaning: Hand washing, power spraying, decontaminating paintwork, cleaning windows, and dressing tyres. * Vehicle Preparation: Ensuring new and used vehicles meet showroom or dealership standards for customer delivery. * Machine Operation: Learning to use professional equipment, including steam cleaners, wet/dry vacs, and orbital polishers. * Site Maintenance: Maintaining the valeting bay, storing equipment, and managing chemical stocks. * Driving Duties: Moving vehicles safely around the lot and assisting with customer collections/deliveries. * Inspection: Checking for vehicle damage before and after the valet process. **Requirements \& Qualifications** * Driving License: A full, clean driving licence is required to move customer vehicles. * Attitude: Hardworking, eager to learn, and high attention to detail. * Fitness: Physically fit and able to handle a demanding, active role that involves bending and reaching. * Experience: Previous experience is not required, as training will be given but a passion for cars is highly regarded. **Learning \& Development** * On\-the\-Job Training: Working alongside experienced professionals to learn proper chemical handling, safety procedures, and cleaning techniques. * Career Progression: Opportunity to progress to a Senior Valeter, Detailer, or move into other automotive roles. * Standards: Developing an understanding of compliance with Health \& Safety regulations. **Typical Working Conditions** * Location: The role involves working both indoors in a valet bay and outdoors, requiring tolerance to various weather conditions. * Hours: 8:30am\-5pm (Mon, Tue, Thur \& Fri), 8:30am\-4pm (Wed). * Environment: Fast\-paced, busy dealership or workshop environment. * Physical Work: Involves standing for long periods and using heavy or awkward equipment. **Ability to commute/relocate:** * Ballymount, County Dublin: reliably commute or plan to relocate before starting work (required) Pay: From €30,000\.00 per year Benefits: * Company events * On\-site parking Work Location: In person

title: Kubernetes Engineer
description: **Overview** We are seeking a Kubernetes Engineer to join our global infrastructure team and help scale, automate, and secure our container orchestration environments across on\-premises and public cloud platforms. As a Kubernetes specialist, you’ll work closely with DevOps, SRE, and security teams to deliver reliable, self\-service, and production\-ready Kubernetes clusters that power mission\-critical applications. **Key Responsibilities** **Cluster Management** * Deploy, manage, and upgrade Kubernetes clusters using tools like kubeadm, EKS, AKS, GKE, or Rancher. * Implement robust RBAC, network policies, ingress controllers, and security frameworks in Kubernetes. **Automation \& IaC** * Automate cluster provisioning and application deployment pipelines using Terraform, Helm, and ArgoCD. * Build reusable modules for consistent infrastructure delivery across staging and production. **CI/CD Integration** * Integrate Kubernetes with modern CI/CD workflows for rapid, safe application delivery. * Support GitOps practices and continuous deployment automation. **Monitoring, Logging \& Troubleshooting** * Implement observability for Kubernetes using Prometheus, Grafana, Loki, and Fluentd/Fluent Bit. * Troubleshoot performance issues, failed pods, memory leaks, and cluster degradation events. **Cloud \& Hybrid Deployments** * Operate Kubernetes workloads across AWS, Azure, GCP, and hybrid/on\-prem environments. * Use tools like Velero, Kasten, or Stash for backup/restore strategies in Kubernetes. **Collaboration \& Support** * Partner with application developers, SREs, and security teams to implement best practices. * Serve as a technical advisor on cloud\-native architectures and containerization s **Experience/Qualification Required** * Certified Kubernetes Administrator (CKA) – Highly preferred * Certified Kubernetes Application Developer (CKAD) * Certified Kubernetes Security Specialist (CKS) * AWS Certified DevOps Engineer – Professional * Google Professional Cloud DevOps Engineer * HashiCorp Certified: Terraform Associate * Linux Foundation Certified System Administrator (LFCS) **Nice to have:** * Experience with GitOps tools (ArgoCD, Flux). * Observability tools like Prometheus, Grafana, ELK/EFK stack. * Experience with service mesh technologies (Istio, Linkerd). * Open\-source contributions in Kubernetes\-related projects * Spectro cloud, Incus, Cloud 9 etc… **About InterSystems** InterSystems, a creative data technology provider, delivers a unified foundation for next\-generation applications for healthcare, finance, manufacturing, and supply chain customers in more than 80 countries. Our data platforms solve interoperability, speed, and scalability problems for large organizations around the globe to unlock the power of data and allow people to perceive data in imaginative ways. Established in 1978, InterSystems is committed to excellence through its 24×7 support for customers and partners around the world. Privately held and headquartered in Boston, Massachusetts, InterSystems has 38 offices in 28 countries worldwide. For more information, please visit **InterSystems.com** .

title: Lawyer
description: **Job Title:** Lawyer **Department:** Legal **Contract Type:** Permanent **Location:** Dublin, Ireland (Hybrid) **Why work with us?** We are the bank for a changing world and we aim to build positive change together: * We are a team of colleagues who are enthusiastic about what we do and the impact our work has on our clients. We work in a highly collaborative, open\-minded environment that embraces innovation and creativity * We offer competitive remuneration and a comprehensive benefits package, including a discretionary bonus scheme, company pension contribution, comprehensive private healthcare packages, health and fitness subsidy, work\-from\-home subsidy, above statutory days of annual leave and numerous other benefits. * We are strongly committed to wellbeing and work\-life balance, including a 50% hybrid work model (where the role allows), paid family leave, fertility and menopause support — available to support all life stage needs. * Equality, diversity and inclusion are at the core of our culture, and we actively promote equal opportunities through creating or being part of initiatives and programmes: * Aspire Female Leadership Programme, provides development and exposure to high\-potential women in the bank, through targeted workshops, executive coaching and formal mentoring * Proud members of 100 Women in Finance and among the first signatories of Ireland’s Women in Finance charter * Joined the \#JamaisSansElles (Never Without Her) Charter, committing its executive committee to only join public or media events with panels of three or more speakers, if at least one of those speakers is a woman * Development and mobility are actively encouraged. BNP Paribas’ culture is built on purposeful talent development and recognition programmes that foster engagement, inclusive leadership and continuous growth. A few of our programmes include: * Leaders for Tomorrow, a programme that fast\-tracks high\-potential talent through training, development and cross\-functional projects, preparing them for future leadership positions * Local and Global Mentoring initiatives, that pair employees with senior leaders, providing staff with platforms to gain advice on career growth topics * Ambassador Awards employee recognition scheme provides rewards to those who have embodied the Bank’s core values * Local Experts (Champions) Programme, identifying internal subject\-matter experts from specific business areas, who excel at their craft * We are a global company operating in over 60 countries with over 180,000 employees from over 150 nationalities. In Ireland we have approximately 550 employees across Dublin and Galway, working in Corporate Institutional Banking (CIB) and BNP Paribas Security Services (BP2S) and Greenval **Equality and Diversity** BNP Paribas is an equal opportunities employer and proudly cultivates a diverse workforce. We believe that diversity of experience enhances our service offering to clients and leads to a better working environment. We welcome applications from the best candidates regardless of age, ethnicity, gender, gender identity, nationality, disability, sexual orientation, socio\-economic background, parental and caring status, or religious belief. Our Employee Resource Groups (ERGs) play a key role in promoting diversity and inclusion through initiatives, events and advocacy programmes that foster a sense of belonging for all colleagues. In BNP Paribas Ireland, we have dedicated networks for parents and caregivers, LGBTQA\+, staff from diverse cultural and religious backgrounds, alongside forums to discuss CSR and gender related topics. **Sustainable Finance** BNP continues to demonstrate the Bank’s steadfast commitment to supporting its clients in their growth ambitions by expanding market reach across assets, delivering excellence in bond markets, and showing the way in sustainable financing. **Role** The Dublin Legal team (CIB), comprises the Head of Legal and 6 other lawyers, a company secretary and support staff. It serves a broad spread of CIB businesses with a strong focus on supply chain management and Transaction Banking (including corporate lending and flow banking products such as Cash Management). We require an Irish or English qualified lawyer on a full\-time basis to join the legal team to support the growing business lines and various functions in the territory. **Key Responsibilities** * Participation in the Financing Legal Platform, including project and transactional support as required. * Support the Supply Chain Management (“SCM”) Group through each of the SCM teams around the world, particularly Dublin, Paris and London, in documenting their transactions and developing robust structures. * Working on the Payable and Receivable structures including analyses of novel electronic platform solutions for the GTS Supply Chain Team. * Working on the implementation of any Bank led projects * Monitoring any developments in legislation and identification of legal risk in the territory and reporting into the SARA tool. * Answering ad hoc queries on NDA’s or any Account opening or onboardings. * Assisting Compliance with sanctions papering and or escalations. * Advising on day to day general corporate law matters, primarily corporate governance queries, arising from the Bank’s activity and the Bank’s subsidiaries activities. * Queries on deposit and current accounts and any new investment and loan products, including marketing materials, if required. * Contributing to IP/IT legal matters that may be allocated to you. * Liaising with business colleagues within the global banking network in the structuring, drafting, negotiation and closing of transactions for the Bank. * Supporting commercial banking activities including bilateral and syndicated lending and trade services offerings. * Working closely with the global trade services team from a legal perspective, including the drafting and review of supply chain finance transactions, working with third party platforms, risk participations, review and drafting of bank guarantees, stand\-by letters of credit etc. * Dealing with queries on account opening, mandates, deposit and current accounts and any new investment and loan products. * Advising on relevant regulatory requirements relating to the Bank's business and its customers. * Provision of legal advice in relation to corporate governance and the overall legal interests of the Bank. * Leading or participating in various projects being run by the Dublin Legal team or as part of the global Financing Legal Platform. * Keeping up to date on current topics affecting the Bank such as ESG, data protection, PSPs, Accelerated Payment products etc. * Drafting various types of security documents including account charges, guarantees etc. including any filings or perfection steps. * Instructing external legal counsel on certain matters falling outside of the day to day scope of the Dublin Legal team. **Miscellaneous** * Formulation and review of legal policies: identifying initiatives for improvements in the implementation of the legal work of the Bank, with emphasis on efficiency and legal risk management and operational control. * Monitoring any developments in legislation and identification of legal risk in the territory. * Assisting with the co\-ordination of the Regulatory Watch Group framework for all Irish BNP Paribas entities. * Assisting with the Bank’s pro bono initiatives in EMEA as well as local pro bono projects. * Preparation of training materials and delivery of training to staff across the Bank. * Assisting the Head of Legal with reporting requirements. **Business Skills** **Qualifications \& Experience required for this role** * Risk analysis * Compliance knowledge * Banking law * Civil and commercial / contract law * Legal Knowledge * Business Organisation **Transversal Skills** * Analytical ability * Ability to develop and leverage networks * Ability to conduct a negotiation * Ability to understand, explain and support change **Behavioural Skills** * Client Focus * Ability to collaborate/Teamwork * Ability to synthetise/simplify * Creativity \& Innovation Problem Solving **Other Skills** * Excellent communication skills; * Strong attention to detail; * Strong team working ethic; * Ability to work in a fast paced environment with multiple conflicting deadlines; * To work in an efficient and timely manner, with an emphasis on attention to detail. * Robust yet approachable and collegial nature, with the ability to act calmly and professionally in a demanding and fast moving business environment; * Independent, thorough and commercially minded, and have strong time management and prioritising skills; * Strong legal knowledge, specifically banking, civil and commercial / contract law; * Strong risk analysis and compliance knowledge; * Comfortable and confident advising the business in a demanding environment; * Creativity and innovative problem solving skills; * Ability to understand, explain and support change; * Competent with MS PowerPoint, Excel and MS Word; **About BNP Paribas** At BNP Paribas, we center our values on what drives the company forward. Our driving forces are: * Agility: we want to behave more simply to embrace useful innovation and digital transformation * Client Satisfaction: we believe that our success lies in being the customers’ and clients’ preferred choice. We seek to listen carefully to them and work closely with them * Compliance Culture: We believe in promoting clear rules to foster a strong culture of compliance and ethics * Openness: We promote open\-minded attitudes towards our stakeholders. We aim for everyone in Group to feel included, to have their say and be empowered **Our Strengths Are Based On Four Key Areas** * Stability: we build upon our solid, long\-term oriented management, our diversified and integrated business model and our international footprint * Expertise: We build upon our recognized and expanding knowledge of our teams * Responsibility: We build upon our culture of responsibility and integrity to ever better serve the interest of our customers * Good Place to Work: We foster a stimulating workplace where people are treated fairly and with respect **Management excellence** BNP continues to foster top\-class people management practices within our strong people focused culture. BNP Paribas managers follow the Group’s Management Charter, which emphasises a positive, empowering team culture, encouraging open communication and supporting development and wellbeing. **Compliance Culture** We believe in promoting clear rules to foster a strong culture of compliance and ethics. We would expect any incoming member of staff to respect all financial security related legislation, including anti\-money laundering (AML); countering the financing of terrorism (CFT); international financial sanctions and embargoes (IFS); anti\-bribery and corruption (ABC); KYC; ongoing client related data screening; and transaction monitoring. BNP Paribas have clear policies on these topics which all staff are expected to familiarise themselves with upon successful entry to the Group. Should you require any reasonable accommodation for the interview process, please let us know. By submitting your application, you agree to BNP Paribas completing a background check and screening in advance of any potential offer being made.

title: Legal Entity Analyst, International Controllership
description: **Minimum qualifications:** * Bachelor's degree in Accounting or Finance, or equivalent practical experience. * Professional qualification CIMA/ACA/ACCA or equivalent. * 4 years of experience in accounting or a related function, or 1 year with an advanced degree in accounting, business, or finance. **Preferred qualifications:** * Experience managing project planning, status reporting, timeline estimation, issue tracking, meeting facilitation, and project documentation. * Experience in agreement and spend governance. * Experience in SQL/ERP and ability to have strong attention to detail, organizational skills and focus on continuous improvement. * Ability to propose next steps and roadblock solutions and anticipate questions and challenges early, manage time, and follow work plans. * Ability to have a collaborative style and work with cross\-functional and international teams. * Excellent problem\-solving skills and ability to communicate with executive management. **About The Job** The international controllership seeks to ensure that any Alphabet company can do any business in any country. In order to realise this goal and operate in a responsible and controlled manner it is imperative to take a risk based approach and to align resources with jurisdictional and regional requirements. This organization therefore forms an integral part of Alphabet’s global controllership, working closely with our global operations team, professional service providers, in\-country business leaders, and the broader finance, tax and legal teams. We manage the Legal Entity processes that allow Google to expand and operate globally, as well as ensuring an optimal global corporate landscape. As part of the Legal Entity Governance team, your work will be a critical coordination point, directly supporting the creation of new legal entities around the world. You will be assessing the overall legal entity landscape to ensure any dormant entities are assessed for liquidation. You will partner closely with various business areas across multiple product areas including Finance Engineering, Controllership, and other finance and on finance teams across Google to ensure our operations and corporate landscape assessments run smoothly. The name Google came from "googol," a mathematical term for the number 1 followed by 100 zeros. And nobody at Google loves big numbers like the Finance team when providing in depth analysis on all manner of strategic decisions across Google products. From developing forward\-thinking analysis to generating management reports to scaling our automated financial processes, the Finance organization is an important partner and advisor to the business. **Responsibilities** * Examine entity data to evaluate Alphabet's worldwide footprint, ensuring an optimal organizational structure and supporting the implementation of legal entity policies across all Product Areas (PAs) as businesses expand or business needs change. * Manage legal entity data and workflows directly within Introspect, using the platform to deliver operational updates on the progress of new entities and liquidations. * Coordinate with various finance and non\-finance teams across Alphabet to facilitate legal entity set up, operationalization and liquidation. * Pinpoint root causes and suggests actionable improvements for legal entity processes, employing technology to enhance both accuracy and efficiency. * Generate detailed project management materials for entity creation, such as timeline estimates, status reports, and issue tracking. Resolve Project Roadblocks, work with stakeholders to overcome obstacles and ensure successful outcomes for assigned portions of cross\-functional projects. Google is proud to be an equal opportunity workplace and is an affirmative action employer. We are committed to equal employment opportunity regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability, gender identity or Veteran status. We also consider qualified applicants regardless of criminal histories, consistent with legal requirements. See also Google's EEO Policy and EEO is the Law. If you have a disability or special need that requires accommodation, please let us know by completing our Accommodations for Applicants form .

title: Manufacturing Bioprocess Associate
description: **Job Description** An amazing opportunity has arisen for a **Manufacturing Bioprocess Associate** , in Dunboyne. In this shift role (12/7\), you will provide operational support for shop floor operations of the Late Stage and Launch Pipeline, at our new state of the art single use biotechnology facility. At our company, we aspire to be the premier research\-intensive biopharmaceutical company. Our Biologics facility in Dunboyne is a state\-of\-the\-art facility that integrates leading\-edge technology with a dynamic workspace to support seamless connection and collaboration. title: Microsoft Technology Engineer
description: We are seeking a talented and proactive Microsoft Technical Engineer to join our team. This role will be responsible for supporting, maintaining, administering, and enhancing the organisation's Microsoft technology stack across SharePoint, Dynamics 365 CRM, and the Microsoft Power Platform. The role blends technical administration, solution development, and business engagement, enabling users and teams to work more effectively through secure, scalable, and well\-designed Microsoft solutions. A strong focus will be placed on low\-code / no\-code development, continuous improvement, and automation. Reporting to the IT Service Deliver Manager, you will contribute to the support \& development of SharePoint \& MS Dynamics customisations, while also supporting the rollout, governance, and optimisation of Microsoft 365 and Power Platform technologies. This role is ideal for someone who enjoys cloud engineering, thrives in a collaborative environment, and is passionate about modern Microsoft technologies. **Key Responsibilities** **Microsoft Cloud, SharePoint, Dynamics \& Power Platform** * Administer and maintain Microsoft 365, MS Dynamics, SharePoint Online, Power Platform, and Azurebased solutions, including configuration, user management, and system optimisation. * Lead or support the design, configuration, and rollout of SharePoint Online sites, intranet, departmental sites, and collaboration spaces. * Manage SharePoint information architecture, permissions, and governance frameworks. * Develop automated workflows using Power Automate and integrate them with existing systems. * Support the rollout and adoption of integrated technologies such as Copilot and Power BI. * Perform SharePoint maintenance activities including backups, audits, and optimisation. * Provide user support, training, and guidance on SharePoint and Power Platform tools. * Track usage, generate performance reports, and drive continuous improvement. * Regularly audit permissions to ensure access is appropriately governed, minimising the risk of oversharing and unintended access. * Stay current with emerging Microsoft technologies and best practices. **Collaboration \& Continuous Improvement** * Work closely with internal customers to clarify requirements and deliver effective solutions. * Collaborate with crossfunctional teams to drive automation and digital transformation. * Keep up to date with best practices in Microsoft development and cloud technologies. * Support other IT team members to meet departmental service and quality objectives. **Summary of Requirements** **Technical Skills** * Experience of implementing security best practices for managing access and governance in MS Technology Solutions (SharePoint/Teams, MS Dynamics, Power Platform). * Administration and configuration of SharePoint sites, libraries, permissions, and workflows. * Administration and configuration of Microsoft Dynamics 365 architecture, including entities, relationships, security roles, and environments. * Working knowledge of Power Automate for building automated workflows. * Familiarity with SharePoint Online administration tools and capabilities. * Integration of SharePoint, MS Dynamics, and Power Automate with other systems (advantageous). * Knowledge of entire Microsoft stack including Microsoft Purview a distinct advantage. **Experience** * 3\-5 years' experience administering and supporting Microsoft 365, including SharePoint Online, Microsoft Teams, and OneDrive. * 2\-4 years' hands\-on experience administering and configuring Microsoft Dynamics 365, including entities (Dataverse tables), security roles, forms, views, and workflows. * 2\-4 years' experience designing and delivering Power Platform solutions, including Power Apps, Power Automate, and Dataverse, in a low\-code / no\-code environment. * Demonstrated experience auditing, managing, and governing access and permissions across Microsoft platforms to ensure secure and controlled information sharing. * Proven experience working directly with business stakeholders to gather requirements, deliver solutions, and support adoption in a production environment. * Strong experience with Microsoft technologies including Azure, Intune, MS365\. **Additional Skills** * Strong communication and interpersonal skills, including the ability to train and support internal users. * Ability to analyse business needs and translate them into actionable technical solutions. * Strong problemsolving and analytical skills with the ability to troubleshoot and optimise performance. * Commitment to continuous learning and staying updated with Microsoft technologies. **Desirable Skills** * Microsoft specialist certifications relating to Power Platform, Dynamics 365, Microsoft 365, or Azure are highly desirable, with support provided for ongoing professional development. * Experience with Teams, OneDrive, or other Microsoft 365 tools. * Microsoft Security, Compliance, and Identity Fundamentals certification. * Microsoft Power Platform Developer Associate or Microsoft Power Platform Solution Architect Expert.

title: NFT Engineer- On site Nenagh Tipperary IRL
description: **QA \-Non\-Functional Test Engineer \- Payments / POS** 📍 Location: Ireland (project\-based / client site depending on role requirements) About the Opportunity At E\-Frontiers, we have spent over 20 years partnering with innovative companies and supporting professionals throughout their career journey. We work closely with clients across fintech, payments, engineering and enterprise technology, helping connect talent with impactful projects. We are currently seeking two Non\-Functional Test Engineers (1 Senior, 1 Mid\-Level) to join a major international project in the payments and POS ecosystem. This is an exciting opportunity to work on large\-scale, enterprise\-grade payment solutions, validating the performance, scalability, resilience and operational integrity of cloud\-native platforms, Android POS devices and payment ecosystem integrations. About the Project You will be working on advanced POS and payments technology across the full stack, including payment applications, terminal management systems, Android POS devices, payment protocols, downstream integrations and cloud\-native infrastructure. This role is ideal for professionals who want to work in a highly technical, fast\-evolving fintech environment with real impact on mission\-critical systems. Key Responsibilities * Define, refine and validate Non\-Functional Requirements (NFRs). * Design and execute load, stress, spike, endurance and soak tests. * Measure key KPIs such as throughput, latency, resource utilisation and SLA adherence. * Validate transaction performance across payment ecosystems. * Test CPU, memory, battery and thermal performance on Android POS devices. * Validate responsiveness, session stability and long\-duration device performance. * Execute extended soak tests on production builds. * Validate Kubernetes\-based microservices environments. * Test auto\-scaling, failover, resilience, deployment strategies and messaging systems. * Assess queue and distributed systems performance. * Validate TLS, API integrity, rate limiting and secure operational performance. * Ensure PCI\-aligned behaviour and secure logging practices. * Analyse logs, dashboards and tracing tools to identify bottlenecks and anomalies. * Monitor latency, throughput, throttling and recovery patterns. * Build NFT strategies, scripts, KPI dashboards and bottleneck analysis. * Participate in Agile / SAFe environments and continuous improvement initiatives. Core Skills \& Requirements * Strong experience in **Non\-Functional Testing / Performance Testing** . * Experience with **JMeter, Gatling or Locust** . * Experience with **Kubernetes, Docker and cloud\-native environments** . * Knowledge of observability tools (Grafana, Prometheus, ELK, Splunk, etc.). * API and protocol testing experience. * Scripting with Python or Bash. * Understanding of payments, POS or regulated systems is highly valuable. * Strong communication, collaboration and stakeholder management skills. * 3\-6 years QA experience. * 2\-5\+ years dedicated NFT / performance testing. * Strong cloud\-native and distributed systems expertise. * Leadership in large\-scale performance programmes. * Strong practical knowledge of performance tools and cloud fundamentals. Benefits * International project exposure * Career progression opportunities * Work with advanced payments and POS ecosystems * Learning \& development support * Long\-term career partnership with E\-Frontiers * High\-impact, enterprise\-scale technical challenges

title: Network Engineer - (Ireland Remote)
description: **About Us** IVC Evidensia is the UK and Europe’s leading veterinary care group, made up of over 900 practices and referral hospitals, united by a shared purpose of happier animals, happier owners. At IVC Evidensia, we believe careers should be built without limits. Our people are supported to make more impact, access more career opportunities, and play an active role in their local and professional communities. Through investment in learning, innovation, and wellbeing, we create environments where our teams can grow in confidence, capability, and fulfilment—at every stage of their career. As part of Central Functions, you will be part of the teams that support and enable our network of over 2,500 practices across 20 countries, driving operational excellence, innovation, and strategic impact across the business. Our Technology teams are responsible for the systems and infrastructure that make that work \- everything our healthcare professionals need to deliver world\-class care. From keeping on top of patient records, health plans, client communications, diary management and dispensing, our teams have a lot to think about and need smarter ways to provide the convenience and expertise that modern Pet owners have come to expect. As a Network Engineer at IVC Evidensia, you’ll play a pivotal role in keeping our clinics and business locations connected, secure, and running at peak performance. Your work will combine technical depth, proactive problem‑solving, and expert support. **Key responsibilities include:** Safeguard network reliability and performance across a diverse European estate of clinics and corporate sites. Provide **3rd‑line support** for networking infrastructure, acting as a trusted escalation point for complex issues. Serve as a subject matter expert, supporting and advising colleagues with your deep networking knowledge. Deploy, configure, and maintain a wide range of networking technologies, ensuring consistent standards and high availability. Proactively monitor network performance, capacity, and resilience — identifying and addressing risks before they become problems. Administer and optimise solutions from Fortigate, Dell, and other key vendors to support evolving business needs. Produce clear, user‑friendly documentation tailored to both technical and non‑technical audiences. Manage third‑party suppliers, ensuring timely delivery, quality services, and strong operational outcomes. **What You’ll Bring** We’re looking for someone who is both technically strong and naturally curious — a problem‑solver who thrives in a fast‑moving environment. You'll be covering all Ireland based Practises \& Clinics, therefore a travel to sites would be required on a regular basis. **Your Skills \& Experience** Demonstrable experience in **network design, support, and troubleshooting** within IT infrastructure roles. Strong analytical thinking with the ability to **diagnose and resolve complex network issues**. Hands‑on expertise with **Layer‑2 and Layer‑3 technologies**, including their design, implementation, and optimisation. A **valid driving licence** and willingness to travel across Ireland for site\-visits. Relevant industry certifications such as **CCNA, NSE4, or NSE5** (highly advantageous). **What’s in it for you?** At IVC Evidensia, we’re committed to supporting your development and wellbeing. When you join us, you’ll benefit from: A role with real business impact supporting teams across the organisation Clear career pathways with progression opportunities within Central Functions and beyond Ongoing learning and development supported by tailored programmes and resources A collaborative, values\-led culture focused on care, innovation, and continuous improvement Flexible working with a remote first working policy **Benefits** Healthcare Cash Plan Cycle to Work scheme Green Cars salary sacrifice scheme Voluntary benefits: choose from a range of benefits to suit you Discounted staff pet care Access to discounts/cashback with hundreds of participating retailers **Diversity, Equality, Inclusion and Belonging** At IVC Evidensia we are committed to Diversity, Equality, Inclusion and Belonging, we are keen to hear from candidates from all minority and diverse groups. As a Disability Confident Employer, we are keen to hear from candidates with disabilities and long\-term health conditions and would be happy to discuss any reasonable adjustments needed during the recruitment process. We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

title: People Technology Integration Engineer (Workday)
description: **Why PlayStation?** PlayStation isn’t just the Best Place to Play — it’s also the Best Place to Work. Today, we’re recognized as a global leader in entertainment producing The PlayStation family of products and services including PlayStation®5, PlayStation®4, PlayStation®VR, PlayStation®Plus, acclaimed PlayStation software titles from PlayStation Studios, and more. PlayStation also strives to create an inclusive environment that empowers employees and embraces diversity. We welcome and encourage everyone who has a passion and curiosity for innovation, technology, and play to explore our open positions and join our growing global team. The PlayStation brand falls under Sony Interactive Entertainment, a wholly\-owned subsidiary of Sony Group Corporation. **Role Overview:** The **People Technology Integration Engineer, Workday** is responsible for designing, building, and evolving integration and data solutions across SIE’s global Workday platform and broader HR technology ecosystem. This role operates as an independent contributor within People Technology Services, owning the design and delivery of scalable, secure, and reliable system integrations. The role focuses on enabling seamless data movement, system connectivity, and interoperability across platforms, ensuring that integrations are maintainable, performant, and aligned with architectural standards. Working within an Agile product delivery model, this role contributes to both the continuous evolution of integration capabilities and the stability of core data flows. The Integration Engineer partners closely with Platform Engineers, Product Managers, and Data teams to deliver cohesive, end\-to\-end technology solutions. This role is suited for individuals with strong technical depth who are motivated to build and improve integration architecture and data pipelines within an enterprise environment. **What you'll be doing:** **Integration Engineering \& Solution Design** * Design, build, and implement scalable integration solutions across the Workday platform and connected systems, including: * Workday Studio * Core Connectors and EIBs * API\-based integrations (REST/SOAP) * Define and apply integration design patterns that promote scalability, reusability, and maintainability * Design secure and reliable data flows, including transformation logic, orchestration, and error handling * Evaluate integration approaches and make informed design decisions, balancing business needs, system constraints, and
